postData.contentHtml:
Wellington, New Zealand – Residents in the capital city of Wellington awoke to a grim scene recently, as an overnight storm led to a catastrophic wastewater failure, flooding homes with raw sewage and sanitary products. The incident, triggered by blocked wastewater pipes, underscores a critical infrastructure challenge facing the city.

The deluge of contaminated water affected numerous low-lying areas, prompting authorities to issue urgent evacuation advisories. With heavy rains continuing to drench Wellington, the risk of further flash flooding remains high, adding to the distress of already impacted communities. The city's utilities company is working to address the extensive damage and restore services, but the immediate aftermath has left many homes uninhabitable and a significant clean-up effort underway.

This event highlights the vulnerability of urban infrastructure to extreme weather conditions and the pressing need for resilient wastewater management systems in the face of changing climate patterns.
postData.title: Wellington's Wastewater Woes: Capital Flooded After Storm
postData.summary: An overnight storm in New Zealand's capital, Wellington, led to widespread flooding of homes with raw sewage and sanitary products, prompting evacuation warnings amidst continuous heavy rainfall.

sources.title: New Zealand capital flooded with feces after wastewater failure
Some residents in New Zealand's capital urged to evacuate due to further flood risk
